name: Agatha Christie
description: The Queen of Crime — her novels, detectives, and legacy.

questions.q: Which fastidious, mustachioed Belgian detective is Agatha Christie's most famous creation?

source.notes: Debuted in 'The Mysterious Affair at Styles' (1920).

questions.q: Which elderly amateur sleuth from St. Mary Mead is Christie's other great detective?

source.notes: Jane Marple, first in 'The Murder at the Vicarage' (1930).

questions.q: Which Christie novel takes place aboard a transcontinental train stranded in a snowdrift?
Which Christie novel is often cited as the best-selling mystery ever written?
Which Christie novel has Poirot solving a murder on a steamer along a famous African river?
Hercule Poirot is famously from which country?
In 1971, Agatha Christie was honored by the Queen with which title?
choices: Dame Commander of the British Empire

source.original_a: Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire (DBE)

questions.q: Which 'most enduring' Christie play has been running in London's West End since 1952?

source.notes: Longest-running stage play in world history.

questions.q: What was the title of Christie's very first published novel?
choices: The Man in the Brown Suit

questions.q: Who is the trusty, often dim-witted military friend who narrates many early Poirot stories?
Tommy and Tuppence — the husband-and-wife duo — first meet in which Christie novel?
choices: By the Pricking of My Thumbs

questions.q: Miss Marple debuted in novel form in which 1930 mystery?

source.notes: Marple first appeared in short stories in 1927.

questions.q: Which English seaside town was Agatha Christie's birthplace and lifelong holiday retreat?

source.notes: Her Greenway estate in Devon is now a National Trust property.

questions.q: Christie's second husband had a profession that inspired her many Middle-Eastern-set novels. What was it?
Which eccentric mystery writer and amateur sleuth — sometimes a fictional stand-in for Christie herself — recurs in Christie's later novels?
Which Christie short-story detective presents himself as a 'psychological consultant' for unhappy people?
In December 1926, Christie famously did what, making national headlines in Britain?

source.notes: Famed Harrogate disappearance following her first husband's affair.

questions.q: The 1974 film 'Murder on the Orient Express' cast which actor as Hercule Poirot?
Which actor portrayed Hercule Poirot across the ITV series 'Agatha Christie's Poirot' from 1989 to 2013?

source.notes: Suchet played Poirot in all 70 televised stories.

questions.q: Christie's 'The Murder of Roger Ackroyd' (1926) shocked readers because of what narrative choice?

source.notes: Dr. Sheppard narrates — and did it. Still cited as one of mystery's all-time twists.

questions.q: Christie published one novel under a pseudonym before revealing her identity. Which pseudonym was it?

source.notes: Six Mary Westmacott romantic novels, starting with 'Giant's Bread' (1930).

questions.q: The 1974 film 'Murder on the Orient Express' was directed by which British filmmaker?

source.notes: Sidney Lumet directed; Richard Rodney Bennett scored.
